checks.detail: instruction.md, task.toml, environment/Dockerfile, and tests/test.sh all present.
solution/solve.sh, oracle.yaml, and job.yaml all present.
allow_internet=false, agent.timeout_sec=7200, verifier.timeout_sec=600, build_timeout_sec=600, agent.user=agent.
git and tmux are installed in the Dockerfile.
tests/ contains extra assets (compute_reward.py, harness.py, hidden_cases/, perf_data/, visible_cases/) that should live in environment/tests/.
reasons: compute_reward.py in tests/
harness.py in tests/
hidden_cases/ directory in tests/
perf_data/ directory in tests/
visible_cases/ directory in tests/

checks.detail: No stray files at root; qa-logs/ and results/ are ignored per rules, jobs/ and .env are gitignored.

notes: Move compute_reward.py, harness.py, hidden_cases/, perf_data/, and visible_cases/ from tests/ to environment/tests/ and COPY them into /tests in the Dockerfile so the tests/ task-root directory only contains test.sh.
Note: environment/ already contains test-suite/ which appears to duplicate visible_cases/ — verify the Dockerfile bundles the correct hidden assets at build time once tests/ is slimmed.

checks.detail: task.toml sets [agent] user = "agent" (line 13).
All required isolation primitives are present in the Dockerfile.
reasons: present: useradd agent (line 40)
present: chown -R agent:agent /app (line 55)
present: chown agent:agent /solution (line 47)
present: mkdir /tests && chmod 700 /tests (line 44)
present: strace installed (line 13), plus procps/file/gcc/make for verifier
present: reference cmark installed root-only under /opt/verifier_tools (chmod -R go-rwx)
uid 1000 implicit via first useradd (consistent with test.sh comment 'agent (uid 1000)')

checks.detail: test.sh locks /logs/verifier first then wipes, sandboxes every agent-code execution under su agent + strace, and always exits 0.
reasons: present: chmod 700 /logs/verifier (line 54) BEFORE rm -rf (line 55)
present: /tests re-locked to 0700 + go-rx stripped on visible/hidden/perf dirs (lines 61-62)
present: env sanitisation (unset LD_PRELOAD/LD_LIBRARY_PATH/PYTHONPATH/... line 67) and clean PATH
present: build phase wrapped in strace -f + timeout 300 + su agent (lines 215-219, 225-229)
present: correctness phase wrapped in strace -f + timeout 480 + su agent (lines 287-290)
present: perf traced run wrapped in strace -f + timeout 60 + su agent (lines 368-372)
present: reward_tamper_in (openat O_WRONLY/O_RDWR/O_CREAT on reward.{txt,json}) checked on every strace log
present: external_converter_execve tripwire on correctness + perf strace logs
present: scoring via compute_reward.py runs as root OUTSIDE strace (lines 301-303 grade, 400-407 reward)
present: no chown root:root on /logs
present: exit 0 on every path (fail_with line 111, success line 428)
present: fallback reward 0.0 via fail_with -> compute_reward.py --fail
present: no set -euo pipefail (intentional, documented at lines 13-14)
present: pre-verification pkill -9 -u agent + survival check (lines 146-153)

checks.detail: runs_agent_code: verifier compiles and executes the agent's md2html C binary, so strace + su agent are required and properly applied.
reasons: verifier builds agent sources via make/gcc and runs the resulting md2html on staged inputs
every agent-code invocation (build, correctness, perf-traced) runs as su agent inside strace -f with a timeout
scoring layer (harness.py grade, compute_reward.py) only reads files; never imports /app code

checks.detail: Task is C-based with no Python/JS/Rust application dependencies requiring a lockfile.
No Python/pip usage; only apt-installed system packages and a cmark build from source.
task.toml has no docker_image field set — expected during development, must be set before final delivery.
reasons: docker_image not present in [environment]

checks.detail: cmark is fetched via 'git clone --depth 1' with no commit SHA, so the reference implementation version can drift across rebuilds.
reasons: Dockerfile line 25 pulls HEAD of commonmark/cmark

notes: Pin cmark to a specific commit SHA in the Dockerfile to guarantee reproducible verifier behavior.
Set [environment].docker_image to a prebuilt registry image before final delivery.

checks.detail: Deliverable (md2html binary at /app/workspace/md2html), stdin/stdout interface, build commands, and scope are all explicit.
Explicit 'You have 2 hours. Build, test, and iterate.' at the end.
Direct and pragmatic with helpful 'tricky areas' hints, reads like a colleague handoff rather than a formal spec.
124 lines, slightly over the 100-line guideline; the CommonMark section list and test-runner bash snippet could be trimmed or linked.
reasons: instruction_lines=124 exceeds 100

checks.detail: No mention of scorer, verifier, reward, or test.sh; 'held-out inputs' is acceptable framing for hidden test corpus.
make, gcc, and python3 (for JSON-driven test loop) are all referenced with example commands.

notes: Consider trimming the bullet duplication between 'What You Must Implement' (lines 35-50) and 'Inline-level structures' (lines 52-60) to bring length under 100 lines.

checks.detail: Starter md2html.c emits empty string so correctness=0 and perf is gated to 0, yielding composite 0.0.
All identified vectors are closed by /tests 0700, agent-uid execution, strace tripwires, and root-only cmark; remaining gaps are well-mitigated.
Every build and every md2html invocation is wrapped in `su agent -s /bin/bash -c 'env -i ...'`.
strace -f traces build, correctness, and one perf run per file; logs are scanned for reward openat and external-converter execve before scoring.
compute_reward.py runs `python3 -ISs` from /tests, takes only CLI counters, never reads /app or imports agent code.
fail_with always writes reward 0.0 via compute_reward.py and exits 0; build/strace/perf steps are bounded by `timeout`.
Baseline emits empty string and matches only 1/385 expected outputs (~0.0026 correctness, ~0.0013 gated perf, composite ~0.002 << 0.01).
Correctness is byte-equal vs CommonMark reference HTML and perf is wall-time vs cmark with 5x slack, so any valid C inline implementation scores equally.
Performance is gated by correctness (good), but correctness is NOT gated by performance under arithmetic-mean aggregation, so a correct-but-slow solution still scores 0.5 with zero perf.
reasons: weighted_sum allows a fully-correct but arbitrarily slow converter to harvest the entire 0.5 correctness weight while contributing 0 to perf
consider multiplicative aggregation or power-mean(p<0) so weak perf also drags the total down, symmetric with the existing correctness->perf gate

checks.detail: Empty/constant/copy-input outputs fail byte-equal grading on ~all 385 cases, and static + execve tripwires block external Markdown libraries and shell-outs to cmark/pandoc/python.
compute_reward.py runs outside strace as root, takes only CLI counters, and never imports/reads /app, so it is independently re-runnable from the staged manifests and counters.
Both fail_with and the success path write reward.json with score (float), subscores (list of {subtask,score}), and additional_data (dict); failure path includes subscores with zeros.

notes: Consider multiplicative aggregation (composite = correctness * (a + b*perf_raw)) or power-mean(p<0) so a correct-but-very-slow solution does not still earn the full 0.5 correctness weight; current gate is one-directional.
Only 1 perf file (perf_links.md) — add a few more covering emphasis-heavy, escape-heavy, and large-paragraph inputs to make the perf signal less brittle to a single workload shape.

checks.detail: Instruction asks for correctness vs CommonMark reference and speed comparable to production C parser; verifier scores exactly those two (50% correctness vs expected HTML + 50% median-of-3 perf vs cmark).

CommonMark 0.31.2 spec is linked, all in-scope inline sections are enumerated, and stdin/stdout interface + build path are explicit; internal 5x cmark perf threshold is hidden but agent only needs to optimize, not target a number.
Dockerfile installs gcc/make/build-essential; workspace ships Makefile + md2html.c starter matching the documented build command; cmark is correctly root-only and only unlocked on the oracle path.
solution/solve.sh exists (63 lines), writes the oracle marker and installs a cmark-execve wrapper that the verifier permits via token-gated bypass.

117 hidden cases cover the same CommonMark sections as the 268 visible cases (only additional section is 'Soft line breaks', which is explicitly listed as required in instruction.md) — structural variants, no new requirements.
Slim base and cache cleaned, but build-essential already provides gcc and make so listing them again is redundant.
reasons: redundant: 'gcc' and 'make' apt packages are subsumed by 'build-essential'

checks.detail: /solution contains only solve.sh with a clear token-gated cmark wrapper.
Core anti-cheat layout is sound, but several env-sanitisation and chmod steps duplicate protections already provided by `env -i` and the 0700 /tests directory.
reasons: useful: chmod 700 /logs/verifier + wipe (mandatory)
useful: re-locking /tests after Harbor upload resets it to 0755
useful: strace tripwires (reward openat + external-converter execve)
useful: static anti-cheat source scan and ELF check
useful: oracle-marker token gate
useful: pkill -u agent before verification (cross-phase isolation)
useful: workspace snapshot for forensics
useful: relax_reward_perms for local-Docker bind-mount reader
redundant: chmod -R go-rx of /tests/{visible_cases,hidden_cases,perf_data} once /tests itself is 0700 (agent cannot traverse parent)
redundant: unsetting CFLAGS/CC/CXX/MAKEFLAGS when every build step already runs under `env -i`
redundant: `find /tests -name '*.sh' -exec chmod +x` since test.sh is the only .sh file in /tests

checks.detail: No files exceed 30MB; largest assets are tests/perf_data (1.5MB) and the test-suite JSON corpora (~1MB each).
tests/visible_cases/ is a byte-identical duplicate of environment/test-suite/, and tests/{hidden_cases,perf_data}/ should live in environment/tests/ per the Harbor layout rule.
reasons: redundant: tests/visible_cases/ (268 files, 1MB) duplicates environment/test-suite/ exactly — harness.py could read /app/test-suite at verification time instead
layout: tests/hidden_cases/ and tests/perf_data/ should be baked into the image via environment/tests/ rather than uploaded at verify time
layout: tests/harness.py and tests/compute_reward.py should live in environment/tests/ so they ship in the image (chmod 700) rather than being uploaded fresh each trial

notes: Drop redundant apt packages (gcc, make) from the Dockerfile.

Move tests/{harness.py, compute_reward.py, hidden_cases, perf_data} into environment/tests/ and delete tests/visible_cases (read from /app/test-suite instead) so tests/ contains only test.sh per the Harbor layout rule.
Trim three redundant prologue steps from test.sh (recursive chmod, compiler env unsets, *.sh chmod find) — none add defence beyond the 0700 /tests dir and `env -i`.

reproducibility.package_manager: none (C task; apt + source build of cmark)

instruction_quality.task_name: CommonMark inline-level Markdown to HTML in C
instruction_quality.task_summary: Implement a from-scratch C program that converts CommonMark Markdown (inline-level constructs: emphasis, code spans, links, images, autolinks, raw HTML, entities, escapes, line breaks) from stdin to HTML on stdout. The binary must build with the provided Makefile and is validated against a 268-case visible test suite plus held-out cases.

measures: /tests chmod 700 root-only at runtime (re-locked in test.sh prologue)
/logs/verifier chmod 700 then rm -rf as first action
cmark reference installed in /opt/verifier_tools with chmod go-rwx (agent EACCES)
Output files pre-created 0666 in root-owned 0755 staging dir (agent can write but not unlink/symlink-swap)
su agent -s /bin/bash -c 'env -i ...' wraps every build and md2html invocation
pkill -9 -u agent + verification of no surviving agent procs before verification
strace -f traces clone/fork/execve/openat over build, correctness, and one perf run per file
reward_tamper_in greps for openat of reward.{txt,json} with O_WRONLY|O_RDWR|O_CREAT
compute_reward.py runs python3 -ISs from /tests with no /app reads, only CLI counters
harness.py grade uses O_NOFOLLOW + S_ISREG + st_nlink<=1 FD-pinned reads of agent outputs
Performance score multiplied by correctness rate (smooth gate)
Env stripped (LD_PRELOAD, PYTHONPATH, BASH_ENV, CFLAGS, CC, etc.) and PATH pinned
Static anti-cheat scan for #include of md libs, exec/system/popen of converters, wrapper scripts
ELF sanity check via `file` rejects script masquerading as md2html
Oracle marker token (per-task random hex) gates the cheat-skip path; token never exposed to agent or verifier env

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Performance is measured on UNTRACED runs while only one perf run per file is traced; a converter could detect ptrace via /proc/self/status TracerPid and exec a fast external tool only when untraced.
acknowledged_vectors.reason: cmark is locked behind chmod go-rwx (EACCES for agent); pandoc/node/perl/ruby are not installed; only python3 is available and a stdlib-only Markdown impl is slower than a real C parser, so the speedup vector has no fast target in this image.

acknowledged_vectors.vector: tests/ root contains visible_cases/, hidden_cases/, perf_data/, harness.py, compute_reward.py in addition to test.sh, violating the HARBOR_GUIDE 'only test.sh in tests/' convention.
acknowledged_vectors.reason: /tests is chmod 700 root at container start and re-locked by test.sh, so the agent cannot read these even though they ship via docker cp instead of being baked into the image; purely a layout/style concern, not exploitable.

acknowledged_vectors.vector: /logs/agent is writable by the agent throughout the trial; agent traces could be tampered before verification.
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Inherent to the single-container model and explicitly acknowledged by HARBOR_GUIDE; does not affect reward computation, which never reads /logs/agent.

reward_design.formula: composite = 0.5 * correctness_rate + 0.5 * (perf_raw_rate * correctness_rate); correctness_rate = passed / (visible_total + hidden_total); perf_raw_rate = mean over perf files of min(1.0, cmark_median * 5 / agent_median)
reward_design.formula_explanation: Equal-weighted correctness and performance, with performance multiplicatively gated by correctness so a fast-but-wrong binary collapses to ~0 instead of harvesting the perf half.

components.name: correctness
components.metric: byte-equal match against CommonMark reference HTML across 268 visible + 117 hidden inline cases

components.name: performance
components.metric: per-file min(1, cmark_median*5/agent_median) averaged over perf files (currently 1 file)

dimension_analysis.name: correctness
dimension_analysis.notes: 385 strict byte-equal CommonMark inline cases including emphasis precedence, multiple-of-3 rule, link destinations, entity refs, code span normalization.

dimension_analysis.name: performance
dimension_analysis.notes: 5x slack vs cmark on perf_links.md is achievable for any reasonable C implementation; gated by correctness so cannot be gamed by a fast no-op.

redundant_items.why: build-essential already pulls in gcc and make
parent /tests is already mode 0700, so non-root cannot descend regardless of child modes
every build/run step uses `env -i` which wipes the entire environment
test.sh is the only .sh file and Harbor itself makes it executable to invoke it
identical content; harness.py can point --visible-dir at /app/test-suite instead
Harbor convention is for tests/ to contain only test.sh; everything else should be bundled into the image via environment/tests/
